---
title: ""
about:
  template: trestles
  image: Castro_picture.jpeg
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: email
      href: mailto:francisca.castro@uc3m.es
    - icon: github
      text: github
      href: https://github.com/frcastrog
    - icon: cloud
      text: bluesky
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/fcastrog.bsky.social
---

My name is Francisca Castro and I'm a visiting professor in political science at Universidad Carlos III de Madrid. 

Previously, I was a postdoctoral researcher at the International Security and Development Center ([ISDC](https://isdc.org/)). 

I'm also associated to the ERC project [Participation & Representation in the Digital Age](https://www.prd-erc.eu/).

I obtained my PhD in Political Science from Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin, where I worked as a Doctoral Researcher in the Chair of International Politics.

My research interests are intrastate conflict, protests and social movements, state repression, democratic backsliding, and authoritarian legacies.

You can contact me at **francisca.castro@uc3m.es**

---

**News & Updates**

- Our paper **The Mobilizing Potential of Mass Emigration: Experimental Evidence from Honduras** was accepted for publication at the Journal of Politics. You can read the accepted version [here](/research/articles/honduras/The_mobilizing_potential_of_mass_emigration.pdf)

- Our paper [External Cues and Policy Preferences: Rethinking the Drivers of Policy Positions](https://academic.oup.com/ijpor/article/38/1/edaf066/8472064) just got published open access at the International Journal of Public Opinion Research.
